config.title: Course Configuration
config.description: Configure how RAG works for this course
config.readOnly: Read-only: TAs can view but not edit course configuration.
config.contextRatioHelp: How much of the context window is used for RAG chunks vs conversation history
config.strategyParallel: Parallel (fast first token, inject RAG mid-stream)
config.strategyHelp: Parallel starts streaming instantly and injects RAG context when ready. Simple waits for RAG before generating. FLARE retrieves after each sentence using the generated text as the query.
config.embeddingProviderHelp: Local runs on this server with zero latency and no API cost. OpenAI requires an API key. Changing provider requires re-uploading documents.
config.openaiWarning: With OpenAI embeddings enabled, every student question in this course is sent to OpenAI to compute a search vector, in addition to the Cerebras call for the answer. Course materials are embedded once on upload; questions are embedded on every send.
config.embeddingModelHelpSpeed: Speed measured on this server at startup.
config.minScoreHelp: Chunks scoring below this threshold are dropped before being sent to the model. 0 disables the filter (top-K only). Use the RAG tab to preview which chunks pass for a sample question.
config.dailyTokenLimitHelp: Maximum tokens a student can use per day in this course. Set to 0 for unlimited.
config.systemPromptPlaceholder: Optional custom instructions for the AI assistant

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: Create API keys for external integrations (e.g. Moodle plugin). Keys are scoped to this course only.
apiKeys.createdBanner: API key created! Copy it now - it won't be shown again.
apiKeys.empty: No API keys yet. Create one to integrate with external services.

canvas.noticeBullet1: Pulls course files and page content only (no submissions, rosters, or grades).
canvas.noticeBullet2: Your Canvas API token is stored in the Minerva database in plaintext. Revoke it in Minerva or Canvas to disconnect.
canvas.noticeBullet3: Document content is indexed locally in Minerva and excerpts are sent to Cerebras when students ask related questions.
canvas.connectionsDescription: Connect a Canvas LMS course to sync its files into Minerva as documents. You'll need a Canvas personal access token with course file access.
canvas.formTokenHint: In Canvas, go to Account -> Settings -> New Access Token to generate an API token.
canvas.courseIdHelpPrefix: Found in the Canvas course URL: canvas.example.com/courses/
canvas.noConnections: No Canvas connections yet. Add one to start syncing course files.
canvas.filesNoItems: No items found in this Canvas course.

conversations.popularTopicsDescription: Common themes extracted from student messages across all conversations
conversations.conversationsDescription: View all student conversations. Pin good answers to make them visible to all students.
conversations.generalNoteLabel: Add a general note to this conversation
conversations.generalNotePlaceholder: Teacher's note visible to all students when pinned...
conversations.messageNotePlaceholder: Add a teacher's note for this message...

flagKind.extraction_engagement_refused: Student didn't engage with Socratic prompt

documents.title: Documents
documents.description: Upload PDFs and other documents for RAG
documents.confirmSingleBody2: and remove its chunks from the vector index. This cannot be undone.
documents.confirmBulkBody_one: This will permanently delete {{count}} document and remove their chunks from the vector index. This cannot be undone.
documents.confirmBulkBody_other: This will permanently delete {{count}} documents and remove their chunks from the vector index. This cannot be undone.
documents.mbzDescription: Upload a course backup file. Only material that is currently visible to students is imported. Hidden activities, hidden sections, and items gated by group, completion, grade, or profile conditions are skipped. Date-gated items are imported when today is inside the allowed window.
documents.kindDialogOverrideHelp: Picking a value here locks it; the auto-classifier won't overwrite it on future runs.
documents.reclassifySelectedTitle: Re-run the classifier on every selected unlocked document.

knowledgeGraph.title: Knowledge Graph
knowledgeGraph.description: Documents in this course as a graph: each node is a document colored by its classification, each edge is a typed relationship inferred by the linker (a sample-solution pointing to its assignment, two docs grouped into the same week/unit, etc.). Hover a node to highlight its neighbours; expand the list below for the full set of edges with rationales.
knowledgeGraph.rebuildTitle: Re-run the cross-document linker. Use after editing kinds or uploading new docs.
knowledgeGraph.pendingTitle: The linker is catching up on recent ingest / re-classify activity. The graph will update on its own within ~30 seconds.
knowledgeGraph.noDocuments: No documents in this course yet.
knowledgeGraph.notBuiltYet: No edges have been computed yet.
knowledgeGraph.pipelinePending: No edges yet -- the linker runs automatically a few seconds after each ingest. Hold tight or check the server logs.
knowledgeGraph.noEdges: The linker found no confident relationships between docs in this course.

edgeKind.part_of_unit: is in the same unit as

knowledgeGraph.exportTitle: Download nodes and edges as a JSON file (suitable for Gephi / NetworkX import).
knowledgeGraph.rejectTitle: Hide this edge and prevent the linker from re-proposing it. The teacher veto persists across rebuilds.
knowledgeGraph.unrejectTitle: Lift the rejection so the linker can propose this pair again on the next rebuild.

invite.title: Invite Links
invite.description: Generate signed URLs for students to join this course

lti.noticeTitle: What LTI launch passes to Minerva
lti.noticeBullet1: Student identifier (username/eppn or a synthetic ID if the platform does not share it) and, when enabled in the platform, display name. Email is not requested.
lti.noticeBullet2: A user row is created in Minerva on first launch; the student is added to this course as a student.
lti.noticeBullet3: Each launch is shown the data-handling disclosure before they can send a first message.
lti.moodleConfigDescription: Enter these values in Moodle when adding an LTI External Tool to your course.
lti.customParamExplainSuffix: links Moodle users to their Minerva identity. Without it, students launched from Moodle will appear as separate users.
lti.privacyNoteSuffix: , "Share launcher's name" is optional (populates display names).
lti.registrationsDescription: After configuring the tool in Moodle, copy Moodle's platform details here to complete the connection.
lti.copyValuesHint: Copy these values from Moodle's tool registration details.
lti.emptyRegistrations: No LTI connections yet. Configure the tool in Moodle first using the values above, then add the connection here.

members.suggestionsDescription: An external system (e.g. Moodle via LTI) indicated these users should have a higher role. Approve to promote them, or decline to suppress future suggestions for the same role.
members.title: Members
members.description: Manage who has access to this course

playDesignations.title: Play Designations
playDesignations.descriptionSuffix: ). The hourly transcript pipeline will discover any new presentations under each designation and auto-index their transcripts into this course.
playDesignations.empty: No designations watched yet. Add one to auto-index presentations.

rag.searchDescription: Test semantic search against your course documents. Drag the threshold to preview which chunks would be sent to the model.
rag.browserDescription: Browse the chunks extracted from your documents

usage.title: Token Usage
usage.description: Track token consumption per student for billing and monitoring
